title: Return on Investment
event: Reyes Co.'s East Division earned income of $45,000 on average invested assets of $300,000, with sales of $360,000. Compute ROI, profit margin, and investment turnover.

computationPaths.narration: ROI decomposes into two levers: how much of each sales dollar survives (margin) and how hard each asset dollar works (turnover).

memorize.body: ROI = income ÷ average invested assets = profit margin × investment turnover.
Two divisions can hit the same ROI by opposite routes — high margin/low turnover (jewelry) vs low margin/high turnover (groceries).

distractors.feedback: That's profit MARGIN (income ÷ sales). ROI divides by invested ASSETS: 15%.
That's investment turnover in percent terms. ROI is income over assets: 15%.
